title: Aureline extension manifest, effective-permission, publisher-continuity, and policy-pack vocabulary (ADR seed)
description: Seed boundary schema for the extension-manifest row, the effective-permission summary, the publisher-continuity row, and the policy-pack constraint row reserved in docs/adr/0012-extension-manifest-permission-publisher-policy.md. This is an ADR-seed schema: the vocabulary and field set are pinned so the install / update review, permission inspector, publisher-continuity packet, mirror-continuity row, support export, and claim-manifest lanes at a later milestone do not invent extension-shaped fields ad hoc. The eventual extension-manifest crate's Rust types will be the source of truth; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads at this milestone. Adding a new member to any enumerated set, or an additive field, is additive-minor with an extension_permission_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing member is breaking and requires a new decision row. Raw artifact bytes, raw signing-key material, raw policy-bundle bytes, and raw publisher-private data MUST NOT appear in any record; records carry refs only.

extension_permission_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this extension-permission vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

permission_vocabulary_version.description: Integer vocabulary version for the permission-scope enumeration. Bumped additive-minor when a new permission_scope_kind is added.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / claim manifests.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

host_contract_family.description: Frozen host-contract-family vocabulary reserved by the ADR seed. Names the class of host the manifest was written against; the concrete sandbox contract lands in the successor ADR.

artifact_transport_family.description: Frozen artifact-transport-family vocabulary reserved by the ADR seed. Names how the artifact is delivered and verified, independent of what runs it.

registry_source_class.description: Where the manifest came from. Quoted by the mirror-continuity row.

permission_scope_kind.description: Minimum permission-scope vocabulary reserved by the ADR seed. Additional kinds are additive-minor with a permission_vocabulary_version bump; repurposing a kind is breaking and requires a new decision row.

scope_target.description: Typed target for the scope: path prefix (filesystem_*), provider id (ai_provider_access / connected_provider_access), setting id (workspace_settings_*), capability ref (capability_inherit), host-contract ref (execution_context_bind), subscription selector (subscription_subscribe), command id (ui_command_contribute), or credential-handle alias class (secret_handle_use).

scope_constraint.description: Optional narrowing detail. Examples: 'read-only', 'egress to declared hosts only', 'requires step-up', 'bounded to declared setting scope'. Free-form but never raw secret material.

rationale_label.description: Human-legible intent for the scope. Raw user secrets are forbidden.

diff_class.description: Typed diff class per scope entry between the declared and effective permission sets. 'unchanged' means the declared entry survived; 'narrowed' means the entry was reduced (e.g. a path prefix trimmed); 'denied' means the entry was dropped; 'step_up_required' means the entry survives only behind step-up approval.

narrowing_reason_refs.description: Refs to policy-pack constraint rows, dependency markers, or host-context refs that produced the narrowing. Raw bundle bytes never included.

publisher_lineage_state.description: Frozen publisher-lineage-state vocabulary reserved by the ADR seed.

publisher_trust_tier.description: Frozen publisher-trust-tier vocabulary reserved by the ADR seed. 'quarantined_publisher' is a first-class terminal state for emergency-disable bundles.

rotation_reason.description: Frozen rotation-reason vocabulary reserved by the ADR seed.

recorded_by.description: Frozen orphan-recording-route vocabulary reserved by the ADR seed.

policy_pack_constraint_kind.description: Frozen policy-pack constraint-kind vocabulary reserved by the ADR seed. Constraints narrow only; a constraint that would widen beyond the manifest's declared set is denied at load.

narrowing_direction.description: Pinned const. Policy-pack constraints narrow only; widening is denied.

audit_event_id.description: Frozen audit-event-id vocabulary reserved by the ADR seed. Additional ids are additive-minor.

denial_reason.description: Frozen denial-reason vocabulary reserved by the ADR seed. Silent downgrade to a generic 'unavailable' affordance is forbidden; every denial emits its typed reason.

freshness_class.description: Mirrors the ADR-0011 freshness-class vocabulary. A summary whose canonical owner could not be contacted within its refresh window MUST NOT render authoritative_live.

redaction_class.description: Declared redaction class for the record when emitted to an observable sink. Mirrors the ADR-0007 redaction defaults.

manifest_schema_version.description: Version of the extension-manifest schema the row was written against. Distinct from extension_permission_schema_version so the manifest can evolve independently of the boundary export.

extension_identity.description: Opaque namespaced id of the form 'publisher_id/extension_id'. Never reused after retirement.

extension_version.description: SemVer string. Pinning and floors are policy-pack concerns; this field carries the declared value only.

declared_permissions.description: Declared permission-scope entries. The initial frozen scope-kind vocabulary is the minimum an implementation MUST recognise.

target_compatibility_range.description: Range of compatibility the bridge covers. Exact shape is reserved for the successor ADR; the field is present to keep the bridge notes separately addressable.

compatibility_bridge_notes.description: Populated only when host_contract_family = compatibility_bridge; required in that case. Null otherwise.

manifest_capability_binding_refs.description: Refs to capability-lifecycle rows (ADR-0011) the extension binds.

policy_pack_applicable_refs.description: Refs to policy-pack constraint rows whose scope covers this extension.

extension_lifecycle_row_ref.description: Ref to the capability-lifecycle row attached to the extension itself (ADR-0011).

declared_permissions_digest.description: Stable digest of the declared permission set so a reviewer can confirm the manifest has not been tampered with post-publish. Algorithm + value; the raw declared set is not embedded here.

declared_vs_effective_diff.description: Typed diff per scope entry between declared and effective. A review surface that hides this field is denied with review_disclosure_incomplete.

transitive_capability_closure_refs.description: Refs to every capability-lifecycle row the effective set inherits through capability_inherit. One entry per closure member.

dependency_markers_ref.description: Refs to the ADR-0011 dependency markers that contributed a narrowing.

policy_pack_narrowings_ref.description: Refs to policy-pack constraint rows that applied. Policy-pack content is never embedded; only refs.

publisher_identity.description: Never reused across retired publishers. A successor mints a new identity and references the retired row via predecessor_publisher_ref.

publisher_display_label.description: Human-legible label; never the trust authority.

active_signing_key_refs.description: Refs to currently valid signing-key records. Raw key material never carried.

key_rotation_history.description: Ordered list of past signing-key transitions.

delay_window_days.description: Lineage-transfer delay window in days. Transfers fire only after the delay with user / admin notification.

audit_event_refs.description: Ordered refs to publisher-continuity audit events.

policy_epoch.description: Epoch counter (ADR-0008). Constraints are scoped to an epoch so a rolled epoch re-evaluates effective permissions.

target_selector.description: Typed selector body for the target_kind; exact shape is reserved for the successor ADR. Raw bundle bytes never included.

ceiling_label.description: Typed ceiling body: the narrowed value (e.g. 'permission_floor:{filesystem_write=step_up_required}' or 'version_pin:1.4.*'). Exact shape is reserved for the successor ADR.

narrowed_permission_scopes.description: Optional: permission-scope kinds the ceiling narrows. Present when constraint_kind = permission_floor or deny_list.

source_ref.description: Ref to the signed policy bundle. Raw bundle bytes never carried here.
